commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=207770c54db083c5515b28a0736a6bff04be5b6a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `run(ctx)`/`ctx.repair` Doctor health-check contract, a split-check adapter, SDK exports, and converts shell completion, startup maintenance, systemd linger, Policy checks, and related tests to the new repair shape.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows lint calls every check with `repair:false`, while the new startup maintenance check unconditionally returns an info finding. The broader item is a contract migration rather than a reported user bug.
commands.review_followup: Human owner review remains needed for the public Doctor repair contract and the branch is draft/protected; the lint regression is concrete but the broader merge decision is not an automation-only repair lane.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks while making the authoring contract simpler for the rest of the stack.

The primary health-check contract is now one method:

```ts
run(ctx) -> HealthCheckRunResult
```

The main author-facing branch is `ctx.repair`:

- `repair: false`: inspect current state and return every structured piece the check can compute without mutating state: `findings`, `changes`, `effects`, `diffs`, and `warnings`.
- `repair: true`: inspect the same state, return the same structured plan, and apply the repair when possible.

A non-mutating repair plan can return `status: "repairable"`. Applied repair results use `"repaired"`, `"skipped"`, or `"failed"`. The runner decides how to present the returned shape for lint, dry-run, diff, JSON, or normal doctor output.

For analysis-first systems where split logic is clearer, this PR adds `defineSplitHealthCheck({ detect, repair })`. The registry still stores and runs `run(ctx)` checks; the split form is an adapter, not the main runner contract. Policy uses this where detection is the dominant abstraction.

Notes stay presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, and no runner-owned note arrays.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks and the supporting runner path:

- `core/doctor/shell-completion` uses direct `run(ctx)` and returns a repairable plan when `repair: false`.
- `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` uses direct `run(ctx)` for the positional maintenance pass.
- `core/doctor/systemd-linger` remains in split form through `defineSplitHealthCheck` because detection and repair are naturally separate.
- Policy doctor checks are registered through the same `run(ctx)` contract; detect-heavy policy checks use `defineSplitHealthCheck`, while the repairable denied-channel check demonstrates a direct `run(ctx)` plan/apply shape.

Presentation-only checks for Claude CLI, browser readiness, and workspace suggestions remain note-sink based and are adapted into the run contract without turning notes into lint findings unless no note sink exists and the text is actionable.

## Contract Examples In This PR

| Check | Shape | `repair: false` | `repair: true` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/shell-completion` | direct `run(ctx)` | returns findings plus `status: "repairable"`, change text, and a shell-completion effect | calls the existing shell completion repair path with the existing confirm hook |
| `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` | direct `run(ctx)` | returns the positional finding plus `status: "repairable"` and a startup-maintenance effect | runs channel startup maintenance through the structured repair runner |
| `core/doctor/systemd-linger` | `defineSplitHealthCheck` | adapter returns findings plus the repair preview/effect without mutating | calls the existing systemd linger repair path with confirmation |
| `policy/channels-denied-provider` | direct `run(ctx)` | returns denied-channel findings plus the config repair plan/effect | returns the repaired config that disables denied channels when workspace repairs are enabled |

`doctor --lint` and `doctor --fix --dry-run` can both use `run({ repair: false })`; the difference is presentation. Lint consumes findings and exit status. Dry-run/diff can also show changes, effects, and diffs.

Detect-after validation is available but opt-in via the repair runner option. It is not a new default behavior in this PR, so checks are not unexpectedly called a second time unless the caller asks for validation.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for converted checks is:

- write direct `run(ctx)` when the check naturally builds a plan and then optionally applies it
- use `defineSplitHealthCheck({ detect, repair })` when detection is the higher-order concern
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- make `doctor --fix --dry-run` reuse `run({ repair: false })` output instead of inventing a separate path
- return `status: "repairable"` for non-mutating repair plans
- keep notes on `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, not in findings or repair payloads

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `run(ctx)` plus `ctx.repair` is the right main contract for doctor repair authors
- whether `defineSplitHealthCheck` is the right adapter for policy-style checks
- whether `status: "repairable"` is the right non-mutating plan state
- whether detect-after validation should remain opt-in before becoming a default
- whether dry-run/diff output should be a presentation of the same `repair: false` plan

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Formatter note: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` was not completed in this linked WSL worktree because pnpm attempted dependency reconciliation and aborted with `ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY`; `git diff --check` and focused type/test proof passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR changes the Doctor health-check runner to a `run(ctx)`-first repair contract with `ctx.repair`, `status: "repairable"` preview plans, split-check adapter support, presentation-only notes, dry-run/diff plan output, and opt-in detect-after validation.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on branch `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 6 focused Doctor/Policy test files passed, 98 tests total, covering `run(ctx)` repair application, `repair: false` preview plans, split-check adapter behavior, lint routing, repairable status, opt-in validation, notes, shell completion, startup maintenance, browser residue repair, and policy repair behavior.

Observed result after fix: Converted and adapted checks run through the single `run(ctx)` runner contract; `doctor --lint` can consume findings from `repair: false`, while dry-run/diff callers can consume the broader plan without giving check authors separate lint/dry-run/diff branches.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this contract rewrite; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Formatter note: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` was not completed in this linked WSL worktree because pnpm attempted dependency reconciliation and aborted with `ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY`; `git diff --check` and focused type/test proof passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR changes the Doctor health-check runner to a `run(ctx)`-first repair contract with `ctx.repair`, `status: "repairable"` preview plans, split-check adapter support, presentation-only notes, dry-run/diff plan output, and opt-in detect-after validation.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on branch `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 6 focused Doctor/Policy test files passed, 98 tests total, covering `run(ctx)` repair application, `repair: false` preview plans, split-check adapter behavior, lint routing, repairable status, opt-in validation, notes, shell completion, startup maintenance, browser residue repair, and policy repair behavior.

Observed result after fix: Converted and adapted checks run through the single `run(ctx)` runner contract; `doctor --lint` can consume findings from `repair: false`, while dry-run/diff callers can consume the broader plan without giving check authors separate lint/dry-run/diff branches.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this contract rewrite; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks while making the authoring contract simpler for the rest of the stack.

The primary health-check contract is now one method:

```ts
run(ctx) -> HealthCheckRunResult
```

The main author-facing branch is `ctx.repair`:

- `repair: false`: inspect current state and return every structured piece the check can compute without mutating state: `findings`, `changes`, `effects`, `diffs`, and `warnings`.
- `repair: true`: inspect the same state, return the same structured plan, and apply the repair when possible.

A non-mutating repair plan can return `status: "repairable"`. Applied repair results use `"repaired"`, `"skipped"`, or `"failed"`. The runner decides how to present the returned shape for lint, dry-run, diff, JSON, or normal doctor output.

For analysis-first systems where split logic is clearer, this PR adds `defineSplitHealthCheck({ detect, repair })`. The registry still stores and runs `run(ctx)` checks; the split form is an adapter, not the main runner contract. Policy uses this where detection is the dominant abstraction.

Notes stay presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, and no runner-owned note arrays.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks and the supporting runner path:

- `core/doctor/shell-completion` uses direct `run(ctx)` and returns a repairable plan when `repair: false`.
- `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` uses direct `run(ctx)` for the positional maintenance pass.
- `core/doctor/systemd-linger` remains in split form through `defineSplitHealthCheck` because detection and repair are naturally separate.
- Policy doctor checks are registered through the same `run(ctx)` contract; detect-heavy policy checks use `defineSplitHealthCheck`, while the repairable denied-channel check demonstrates a direct `run(ctx)` plan/apply shape.

Presentation-only checks for Claude CLI, browser readiness, and workspace suggestions remain note-sink based and are adapted into the run contract without turning notes into lint findings unless no note sink exists and the text is actionable.

## Contract Examples In This PR

| Check | Shape | `repair: false` | `repair: true` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/shell-completion` | direct `run(ctx)` | returns findings plus `status: "repairable"`, change text, and a shell-completion effect | calls the existing shell completion repair path with the existing confirm hook |
| `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` | direct `run(ctx)` | returns the positional finding plus `status: "repairable"` and a startup-maintenance effect | runs channel startup maintenance through the structured repair runner |
| `core/doctor/systemd-linger` | `defineSplitHealthCheck` | adapter returns findings plus the repair preview/effect without mutating | calls the existing systemd linger repair path with confirmation |
| `policy/channels-denied-provider` | direct `run(ctx)` | returns denied-channel findings plus the config repair plan/effect | returns the repaired config that disables denied channels when workspace repairs are enabled |

`doctor --lint` and `doctor --fix --dry-run` can both use `run({ repair: false })`; the difference is presentation. Lint consumes findings and exit status. Dry-run/diff can also show changes, effects, and diffs.

Detect-after validation is available but opt-in via the repair runner option. It is not a new default behavior in this PR, so checks are not unexpectedly called a second time unless the caller asks for validation.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for converted checks is:

- write direct `run(ctx)` when the check naturally builds a plan and then optionally applies it
- use `defineSplitHealthCheck({ detect, repair })` when detection is the higher-order concern
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- make `doctor --fix --dry-run` reuse `run({ repair: false })` output instead of inventing a separate path
- return `status: "repairable"` for non-mutating repair plans
- keep notes on `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, not in findings or repair payloads

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `run(ctx)` plus `ctx.repair` is the right main contract for doctor repair authors
- whether `defineSplitHealthCheck` is the right adapter for policy-style checks
- whether `status: "repairable"` is the right non-mutating plan state
- whether detect-after validation should remain opt-in before becoming a default
- whether dry-run/diff output should be a presentation of the same `repair: false` plan

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Formatter note: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` was not completed in this linked WSL worktree because pnpm attempted dependency reconciliation and aborted with `ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY`; `git diff --check` and focused type/test proof passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR changes the Doctor health-check runner to a `run(ctx)`-first repair contract with `ctx.repair`, `status: "repairable"` preview plans, split-check adapter support, presentation-only notes, dry-run/diff plan output, and opt-in detect-after validation.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on branch `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 6 focused Doctor/Policy test files passed, 98 tests total, covering `run(ctx)` repair application, `repair: false` preview plans, split-check adapter behavior, lint routing, repairable status, opt-in validation, notes, shell completion, startup maintenance, browser residue repair, and policy repair behavior.

Observed result after fix: Converted and adapted checks run through the single `run(ctx)` runner contract; `doctor --lint` can consume findings from `repair: false`, while dry-run/diff callers can consume the broader plan without giving check authors separate lint/dry-run/diff branches.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this contract rewrite; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.

`doctor --lint` and `doctor --fix --dry-run` can both use `run({ repair: false })`; the difference is presentation. Lint consumes findings and exit status. Dry-run/diff can also show changes, effects, and diffs.

Detect-after validation is available but opt-in via the repair runner option. It is not a new default behavior in this PR, so checks are not unexpectedly called a second time unless the caller asks for validation.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for converted checks is:

- write direct `run(ctx)` when the check naturally builds a plan and then optionally applies it
- use `defineSplitHealthCheck({ detect, repair })` when detection is the higher-order concern
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- make `doctor --fix --dry-run` reuse `run({ repair: false })` output instead of inventing a separate path
- return `status: "repairable"` for non-mutating repair plans
- keep notes on `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, not in findings or repair payloads

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `run(ctx)` plus `ctx.repair` is the right main contract for doctor repair authors
- whether `defineSplitHealthCheck` is the right adapter for policy-style checks
- whether `status: "repairable"` is the right non-mutating plan state
- whether detect-after validation should remain opt-in before becoming a default
- whether dry-run/diff output should be a presentation of the same `repair: false` plan

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Formatter note: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` was not completed in this linked WSL worktree because pnpm attempted dependency reconciliation and aborted with `ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY`; `git diff --check` and focused type/test proof passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR changes the Doctor health-check runner to a `run(ctx)`-first repair contract with `ctx.repair`, `status: "repairable"` preview plans, split-check adapter support, presentation-only notes, dry-run/diff plan output, and opt-in detect-after validation.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on branch `doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-run-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 6 focused Doctor/Policy test files passed, 98 tests total, covering `run(ctx)` repair application, `repair: false` preview plans, split-check adapter behavior, lint routing, repairable status, opt-in validation, notes, shell completion, startup maintenance, browser residue repair, and policy repair behavior.

Observed result after fix: Converted and adapted checks run through the single `run(ctx)` runner contract; `doctor --lint` can consume findings from `repair: false`, while dry-run/diff callers can consume the broader plan without giving check authors separate lint/dry-run/diff branches.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this contract rewrite; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.
